title: Reading and Use of English - Part 2
description: For questions 9 - 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Mangrove forests are coastal ecosystems found in tropical and subtropical regions, (0) which provide a remarkable range of ecological services that are only now beginning to be fully appreciated. Their importance to biodiversity and climate stability is increasingly well documented. (9) Although mangroves occupy less than one per cent of the total forested area of the Earth, they are disproportionately productive both in terms of carbon sequestration and the marine species they support. (10) Such is the structural complexity of the root systems of mangrove trees that they create nursery habitats for a wide variety of fish and crustacean species that are commercially vital to coastal communities. These coastal forests also provide critical protection from storm surges, and (11) Provided that the mangrove belt is sufficiently wide and dense, it can significantly reduce the impact of wave energy on the shorelines behind it. Not (12) only do mangrove roots trap sediment and prevent coastal erosion, but they also store significantly more carbon per unit area than most terrestrial forest types. (13) Regardless of these recognised benefits, mangrove forests have been lost at a rate of roughly one to two per cent annually over recent decades, primarily due to coastal development and aquaculture expansion. Not (14) until economists began to quantify the financial value of mangrove ecosystem services did the case for their protection gain significant traction in policy discussions. (15) Amid growing recognition of their role in climate mitigation, investment in mangrove restoration has increased substantially in the past decade. Conservation organisations now work alongside local communities to protect remaining forests and replant cleared areas. (16) Whether mangrove conservation is framed primarily as a climate solution or as a biodiversity priority, the urgency of protecting and restoring what remains is beyond question.

example.text: Mangrove forests are coastal ecosystems found in tropical and subtropical regions, (0) which provide a remarkable range of ecological services that are only now beginning to be fully appreciated.

questions.explanation: Concessive conjunction used to introduce a contrast or concession between two clauses.
Emphatic determiner in the inverted structure 'Such was/is the [noun] that...' to express a high degree.
Conditional conjunction meaning 'on the condition that', used to introduce a condition and its result.
Part of the correlative conjunction 'Not only... but also', which requires subject-auxiliary inversion after 'Not only'.
Part of the fixed prepositional phrase 'Regardless of', meaning 'without being affected by'.
Part of the 'Not until' negative fronting structure, which requires subject-auxiliary inversion.
Preposition meaning 'in the middle of' or 'surrounded by a particular situation', used as a temporal and contextual discourse marker.
Conjunction introducing two alternatives or possibilities in a summarising conclusion.
